rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MICHAEL CLAWSON VS. BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Writ
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 16, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER WILLIAM CLAWSON's Motion For Judgment On Writ. Petitioner William M. Clawson's petition for writ of mandate is denied. Petitioner fails to demonstrate that respondent abused its discretion by not "proceed[ing] in the manner required by law." (Code Civ. Proc. § 1094.5(b).) In exercising its independent judgment on the facts, this Court "must apply a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings, and the party challenging the administrative decision bears the burden of convincing the court that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence." (Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 209 Cal.4th 805, 817; Finnerty v. Board of Registered Nursing (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 219, 227.) The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY VS. THE WHITEWAVE FOODS COMPANY A DELAWARE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 16, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY's MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. Plaintiff Erika McCartney's motion for entry of consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Answers And Production From Defendant Murali Ranjithan, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: October 16, 2019 Line 1, PLAINTIFFS GARY PRESLEY-NELSON, BEVERLY PRESLEY-NELSON'S Motion To Compel Deposition Answers And Production From Defendant Murali Ranjithan, M.D. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted as follows: Dr. Ranjithan shall answer questions regarding the 2008 MRI scans, and regarding whether before 2009 anyone had ever criticized his interpretation of a MRI. Dr. Ranjithan shall not be compelled to provide his present or after the fact opinions about the 2008 MRI scan. To clarify: the questions delineated on Plaintiff's Reply Memorandum 2:20-3:23 are proper because they do not seek Defendant's present or after the fact opinions. Defendant shall produce (or state his inability to do so pursuant to CCP 2031.230) any documents in his control from non-party Image Guided Therapeutics, Inc. Defendant's right to privacy in those documents is outweighed by Plaintiff's right to evidence. While the issue is not squarely before the court, it is strongly suggested that the parties cooperate to authenticate any of the documents in question. It is further suggested that, in the interests of judicial economy, the parties cooperate to determine which of any deposition questions can be answered in writing or in a telephone deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: SUZANNE GAMBLE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 16, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant filed its motion on July 19, 2019, on more than the statutorily-required 75 days notice. (Code Civ. Proc. § 437c(a)(2).) On August 2, 2019, for good cause shown and pursuant to the agreement of the parties, the Court ordered that the motion be heard on October 16, 2019, the originally noticed hearing date, less than 30 days prior to the November 4, 2019 trial date. (Code Civ. Proc. § 437c(a)(3).) That order provided that Plaintiff's opposition papers would be due 14 calendar days before the hearing date, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. § 437c(b)(3). Plaintiff has not opposed the motion for summary judgment on its merits or filed a separate statement of material facts in opposition to the motion. Plaintiff is not entitled to special consideration because of her self-represented status. (See ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208.) Accordingly, the motion must be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PITTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 16, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff's second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's late-filed one-page "Opposition to Demure [sic]" does not contain any legal argument or, for that matter, any content at all, other than the bare fact that Plaintiff opposes the demurrer. Plaintiff is not entitled to special consideration because of his self-represented status. (See ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he will not appear at the hearing. The Court will sign the proposed order included with the City's moving papers.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JON B. EISENBERG ET AL VS. DAVE JONES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: October 16, 2019 Line 2, PLAINTIFF JEFF SENGSTACK'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Based on documents attached to the pleadings, it is impossible to see which alleged privileges apply to which document requests. Defendants are obligated to produce a privilege log which relates each document to the specific documents request at issue. See e.g. Hernandez v Sup. Ct. (2003) 4 Cal.Rptr.3d 883, 891 ("The court orally warned petitioners not to use a boilerplate response in order to identify documents, but to relate each document to the specific interrogatory requesting the identification.") Perhaps Defendants have already done so, but the Court cannot find any pleading which specifically ties a given document request to a particular assertion of privilege. In general, the Court finds that the contents of the privilege log are otherwise sufficient to allow a determination of whether each withheld document is or is not in fact privileged. Wellpoint Health Networks, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 59 CA 4th 110, 130. No final determination of sufficiency can be made without tying specific requests to specific alleged privileges. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. The Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted based On Code Section 2033.280.
